metric_value: DEC ran Debit/Credit tests on both DEC and IBM equipment; announced July 1988
notes: Results showed DEC substantial price/performance advantage over IBM.

metric_value: IBM machines perform 3x faster than DEC measurements
notes: IBM response per Computerworld Oct 24 1988; IBM ran only on own hardware (9370/4381).

metric_name: DEC terminal count in Debit/Credit test
metric_value: 1,000 terminals
notes: Per Computerworld source; DEC used full network implementation.

metric_name: IBM terminal count in Debit/Credit test
metric_value: ~100 terminals
notes: IBM claimed mathematical equivalence; minimised network overhead role.

metric_name: IBM systems tested in own Debit/Credit benchmark
notes: IBM used different Debit/Credit implementation from DEC.

metric_name: Aberdeen Group comment on IBM response
metric_value: Pure FUD — IBM must be hurt by the DEC performance figures
notes: Quote from John Logan VP Aberdeen Group; from Computerworld Oct 24 1988.

metric_value: Delayed from October 1988; rerunning tests for external auditor
notes: Also waiting for TPPC final definition of Debit/Credit.

metric_value: By November 1 1988; written by Tom Sawyer (Codd and Date Consulting)
notes: Tom Sawyer senior consultant, San Jose CA.

metric_name: Tom Sawyer auditor comment on benchmark methodology
metric_value: Mathematically proportional but performance could vary widely in implementation
notes: Any scoring in this area is controversial; no certainty about full terminal implementation outcome.

metric_name: TPC formation date and founding members
metric_value: August 10, 1988; CDC, DEC, ICL, Pyramid, Stratus, Sybase, Tandem, Wang
notes: Omri Serlin of Itom International spearheaded TPC formation.

metric_value: Probably a good assumption that IBM will join TPC
notes: Quote from Kenneth MacMorran IBM performance evaluation center Dallas; IBM also considering releasing Ramp C to TPC.

metric_value: 49 TPS on VAX 6240 running VMS
notes: Broke previous VAX/VMS record of 29 TPS on VAX 8700/8800 cluster. Verified by Codd and Date.

metric_name: Oracle world record on IBM MVS (Amdahl 3090-600E)
metric_value: 265 TPS
notes: Broke Tandem 16-node cluster record of 240 TPS. Verified by Codd and Date.

metric_value: 124 TPS on Sequent large minicomputer
notes: Independently verified by Codd and Date Consulting Group.

metric_value: Proprietary IBM benchmark; mix of transactions analogous to many businesses
notes: Contrasted with Debit/Credit which simulates simple ATM-type transactions.

metric_value: Established principles: independent oversight, standardized methodologies, comprehensive disclosure
notes: TPC-A/B/C standards directly resulted from 1988 controversy.

metric_name: Jim Gray assessment of DEC-IBM benchmark discrepancy
metric_value: DEC test on virtually identical IBM model yielded substantially poorer results; additional fuel to benchmarketing wars
notes: Jim Gray original architect of DebitCredit benchmark; from TPC Benchmark Handbook.

metric_value: IBM tested DEC equipment under Ramp C; published results; DEC responded by criticizing Ramp C as proprietary
notes: MacMorran (IBM Dallas) said he was responsible for benchmarks that started the controversy.

metric_value: Originally called Debit-Credit Council; renamed Transaction Processing Performance Council
notes: Omri Serlin of Itom International (Los Altos CA) started the group.

metric_value: Independent auditor certification; full disclosure reports; anti-benchmarketing rules
notes: Direct legacy of 1988 DEC-IBM controversy.
